title: grammitis kirkii
intro: Grammitis kirkii, also known as the Maori button fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand, characterized by its unique button-like fronds. The plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Grammitis kirkii has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
